policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Repeated clean post-review local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-19 each passed all 1738 tests and measured the complete 27746-line assembly scope, with covered lines oscillating one line between 19376 and 19377 (high-water 19377 = 69.837%) purely from previously reproduced Coverlet instrumentation nondeterminism. Issue #98 (BI-PERF-037) removes the per-item N+1 in the Spoiler Guard tag projection: ApplyTagCacheSpoilerStrip now memoizes GetItemById per guid, batch/memoizes played-state, and computes each Season's watched aggregate at most once per relevant revision (reused within a response and across full/delta at unchanged revision, with targeted invalidation on user-state change), resolving item-type/season facts from cached tag-cache entry/index data and honoring a CancellationToken that fails closed. The fail-closed privacy output stays byte-identical to the pure ResolveTagStripDecision. New SpoilerUserResolver and TagCacheProjectionRevisionService helpers plus the TagCacheSpoilerStripProjection/ProjectionRevision suites (manager-call-count budgets for 15k broad-scope and large-series responses, the watched/unwatched x scope parity matrix, per-revision aggregate reuse, and cancellation fail-closed) exercise every added path. Relative to the reviewed 19150/27560 baseline, the reviewed assembly scope grows by 186 instrumented lines and the high-water advances by 227 covered lines while line coverage rises from 69.485% to 69.837%. The existing two-line tolerance is unchanged and covers only the reproduced negligible Coverlet variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
